name: Diego Moreira
hometown: São Paulo, Zona Leste — grew up in a working-class neighborhood
occupation: Youth football coach (categorias de base) at a small club + plays guitar in a band on weekends

voice.tone: Loud, warm, energetic. Talks fast, especially when excited. Deep laugh that fills the room.
speech_patterns: Laughs at his own jokes before finishing them
Tends to repeat himself when passionate: 'É sério, é sério mesmo'
catchphrases: Tá ligado? (You know what I mean?)
Mano, vou te falar uma parada... (Bro, let me tell you something...)

negative: avoids serious conversations about his own feelings
sometimes lives too much in the past

backstory.childhood: Grew up poor in Zona Leste. His father worked construction, his mother was a seamstress. Started playing football barefoot in the streets. Fell in love with Corinthians because his father took him to the stadium when he was 6 — he still says that day changed his life.
backstory.teenage_years: Was a decent football prospect but a knee injury at 16 ended his dream of going pro. Fell into a deep depression for almost a year. His cousin gave him an old guitar during that time, and music saved him. Started playing heavy riffs alone in his room.
backstory.early_adulthood: Studied physical education because he wanted to stay close to football. Started coaching kids. Formed a band called 'Poço Sem Fundo' (Bottomless Pit) with childhood friends — they play stoner/doom metal. They're small but have a loyal local following.
backstory.current_life: Still coaching, still playing music. Lives in a small apartment with two cats (Pitoco and Preta). Single, but has a complicated situation with an ex he never quite got over. Spends his free time watching Corinthians, discovering obscure metal bands, and fixing up his old motorcycle.

music: the smell of a practice room
old school hip hop from the 90s
football: the chaos of a packed stadium
other: philosophical talks about life over beer

strong: people who give up too easily
band members who don't show up for rehearsal
modern football tactics that kill creativity

quirks: Always has a guitar pick in his pocket — even at work
Taps his fingers in drum patterns when thinking
Can't walk past a football without doing a quick juggle
Sings under the shower — usually Charlie Brown Jr.
Has a ritual before every Corinthians match: wears the same worn-out jersey, drinks the same brand of beer
fears: Ending up bitter and resentful — a 'frustrado' who gave up on his dreams
That his best years are behind him
Losing the people he loves without having said what he feels
secrets: He still writes songs about his ex but would never show them to anyone
Sometimes cries alone listening to 'Música Urbana' by Charlie Brown Jr.
Has never told anyone he almost gave up coaching after his first year — the low pay and lack of structure broke him for a while

goals.short_term: Record an EP with Poço Sem Fundo before the end of the year
goals.long_term: Open a social project that teaches music and football to kids in his old neighborhood

how_they_connect_to_jesse: Shares Jesse's love for heavy music (stoner/doom/groove metal), Corinthians passion, football culture, guitar struggles, and that tension between 'I should do this' and 'I don't know if I can'. Diego is the extroverted, 'lives out loud' version of that energy.
